lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this voucher for completing the combat Adventure Path, it says to come talk to you about claiming my Adventure Path reward.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Why yes, here are some energy potions.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I do have more rewards for you, but you need to be on a members world for me to give you the reward. Maybe there is something else I can help you with.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I do have more rewards for you, but you need to be on a members world for me to give you the reward. Maybe there is something else I can help you with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make potions for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Apothecary, I've lost that hat you gave me.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: No problem, I get plenty of these from Gielinor Hospital Supplies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wouldn't be able to make any other cat potions, would you?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I have no plans to branch out into cat potions at the moment, but if there was sufficient demand for them I might change my mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any decent gossip to share?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Well I hear young Romeo's having a little woman trouble but other than that all's quiet on the eastern front. Can I do something for you?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Sad about that affair with young Romeo and Juliet...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I hear every time Romeo sees Juliet now he runs away screaming something about ghosts and Juliet's cousin?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Always did think he was a bit of a strange one...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Anyway! Life goes on and so does business! Can I do something for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know a potion to make hair fall out?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I do indeed. I gave it to my mother. That's why I now live alone.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: But can I do something for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any good potions to give away?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Sorry, charity is not my strong point. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

sections.title: Giving him the Diary (Shades of Mort'ton)

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Do you want to trade the diary yet? I'd like to pass the information on to the scientific community in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll trade it for herblore training.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Great...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Ok, that's all I can teach you. Many thanks for the diary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I want to keep hold if it a bit longer.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Oh, that's a shame... Come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Hmm, this is an interesting tome... But you've already sold me one, thanks!

sections.title: Using cadava berries on him after Romeo and Juliet

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I don't need those any more.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Well done.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Very well done. This is for you.
